title: Winama Naberrie

{{Quote|Fate is a tangle. We can only follow one thread.|Winama Naberrie, as recalled by her granddaughter Padmé|<ref name="Journal" />}}
'''Winama Naberrie''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] weaver who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. In [[77 BBY]], she gave [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] to the [[Human/Legends|Human]] male [[Ruwee Naberrie/Legends|Ruwee Naberrie]], who later [[Marriage/Legends|married]] a woman named [[Jobal Naberrie/Legends|Jobal]] following machinations of Winama and Jobal's mother [[Ryoo Thule]], although the two [[Parent/Legends|mothers]] were both surprised by the successful romance.

In [[46 BBY/Legends|46 BBY]], Jobal gave birth to a daughter, [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Naberrie]], who would later live with Winama in Naboo's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. Padmé became close with her grandmother, although she was often confused by the sayings her grandmother would offer. Winama [[Death/Legends|passed]] away in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], and Padmé, who had been elected as [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Queen]] Amidala of Naboo, wished she could still speak to her while dealing with the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] in the [[32 BBY/Legends|following year]]. With Amidala's [[Wedding of Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala/Legends|wedding]] to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], Winama became connected to the most famous family in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].

==Biography==
===Life===
{{Quote|Her name was Winama. She died last year. She wasn't a farmer like her son, my father. She always preferred city life. I lived with her in Theed during my training. She was a weaver.|Padmé Amidala recalls facts about her grandmother|<ref name="Journal" />}}
[[File:RuweeNaberrie-SWCTP.png|thumb|right|300px|Ruwee Naberrie, Winama's son]]
Winama Naberrie<ref name="Fact 140" /> was a [[Gender/Legends|female]]<ref name="Journal" /> member of [[Naberrie family/Legends|House Naberrie]]<ref name="Databank" /> who worked as a cloth weaver on the<ref name="Encyclopedia">{{CSWECite|Naberrie, Winama|352|II}}</ref> [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="Journal" /> Records of her birthdate and the identity of her [[Marriage/Legends|spouse]] were lost or deliberately obscured at some point in [[Time/Legends|time]].<ref name="Fact 140" /> The weaver had multiple children<ref name="Databank" /> and, in [[77 BBY]], gave [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] to<ref name="Fact 140" /> the [[Human/Legends|Human]] male [[Ruwee Naberrie/Legends|Ruwee Naberrie]] in a [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] [[Unidentified Naboo mountain village (Theed)|village]] not far from Naboo's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] [[Theed/Legends|Theed]],<ref name="Fact 90">{{FactFile|90|NAB 5-6|Ruwee Naberrie}}</ref> where Winama would later live and work.<ref name="Encyclopedia" />

She upheld the Naberrie family's values of generosity and compassion while raising her children,<ref name="Databank" /> which affected Ruwee heavily and led to him joining the [[Refugee Relief Movement/Legends|Refugee Relief Movement]] at the age of ten to help the less fortunate off-world.<ref name="Fact 140" /> Later, Winama conspired with her good friend [[Ryoo Thule]] to have Ruwee marry Thule's daughter, [[Jobal Naberrie/Legends|Jobal]].<ref name="Last">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]''</ref> Ruwee met Jobal on his return to Naboo<ref name="Fact 90" /> and married her in [[51 BBY/Legends|51 BBY]],<ref name="Fact 140" /> with their genuine romance surprising both Winama and Ryoo.<ref name="Last" />

Ruwee and Jobal spent their early married [[Standard year/Legends|years]] in a [[Unidentified Naboo mountain village (Padmé Amidala)/Legends|village]] they helped build in Naboo's mountains<ref name="Fact 90" /> in contrast to Winama's preference for city life.<ref name="Journal" /> They had two children, [[Sola Naberrie/Legends|Sola]], born in [[50 BBY/Legends|50 BBY]], and [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé]], born in [[46 BBY/Legends|46 BBY]].<ref name="Fact 140" /> Padmé scored extremely highly on aptitude tests, causing Ruwee and Jobal to relocate to Theed to broaden their daughters' opportunities.<ref name="Fact 90" /> During Padmé's education, she lived with Winama for a time in Theed, and the two became close, with Winama inspiring her granddaughter with the belief that she could achieve anything. Winama would often provide sayings to Padmé that puzzled her to the point that she grew impatient. One such saying compared fate to a tangle and stated that individuals could only follow a single thread.<ref name="Journal" />

===Death and legacy===
{{Quote|I wish I could talk to her now. I wish I could say, Winama, I think I'm beginning to understand.|Padmé, writing in her journal during the Invasion of Naboo|<ref name="Journal" />}}
[[File:PadmePeasant-DaG.png|thumb|left|250px|Padmé Amidala (pictured) thought about one of her grandmother's sayings while on Tatooine.]]
Winama [[Death/Legends|passed away]] in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]],<ref name="Fact 140" /> and Padmé missed her greatly.<ref name="Journal" /> She was elected [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Queen of Naboo]], taking the formal name of Queen Amidala,<ref name="Padme databank">{{DB|character|amidala|Amidala, Padmé}}</ref> and the [[32 BBY/Legends|following year]] was faced with a planetary [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. After escaping Naboo and arriving on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Amidala thought of Winama's saying about fate while walking to the home of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] [[Youngling/Legends|boy]] she had just met. She later reflected on her relationship with her grandmother in her journal and wished she could talk with her to acknowledge her increasing understanding of the saying. However, Amidala also believed that she could not have bore the thought of Winama interned in a Trade Federation [[Prison/Legends|prison]] camp as her parents had been.<ref name="Journal" />

Amidala later [[Wedding of Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala/Legends|married]] Skywalker, who had become a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], posthumously connecting Winama to what would become the most famous [[Skywalker family/Legends|family]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history. Amidala gave birth to twins, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]], in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Fact 140" /> but she died immediately afterwards. Naboo funeral customs placed grandmothers in charge of dressing the body in preparation for its final journey, and Thule fulfilled that role given Naberrie's passing.<ref name="Last" />

In approximately [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]],<ref name="Readers">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of ''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]'' in approximately [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]].</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Malorum]] investigated Amidala's death. He learned about Naboo's funeral customs and harassed a manager at the [[Naboo Essentials Provider]] into revealing the names of Amidala's grandmothers. When Malorum asked for their addresses, the manager informed him that Naberrie had passed away and only revealed Thule's residential area after being threatened. On the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] that Malorum found Thule, she had been reminiscing about Winama and their successful ploy to marry Ruwee and Jobal.<ref name="Last" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Those are the facts I can tell you about Winama. But the feelings are something else. How close we were, and how funny she was, and how she gave me the feeling I could do anything.|Padmé's recollections of her grandmother|<ref name="Journal" />}}
Winama Naberrie exhibited the values of generosity and compassion that were important to her family,<ref name="Databank" /> passing those values on to her son. Unlike her son,<ref name="Fact 90" /> she had a preference for living in cities,<ref name="Journal" /> and like Padmé, she loved living in Theed.<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> Winama had a close<ref name="Journal" /> and special bond<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> with her granddaughter Padmé, who appreciated her humor and the feeling that she could achieve anything. Winama made a habit of giving sayings that perplexed her granddaughter, who grew impatient in trying to understand their meaning. Winama could tell that Padmé was internally trying to understand what her sayings meant.<ref name="Journal" />

==Skills and abilities==
Winama Naberrie was regarded as one of the best cloth weavers in Theed.<ref name="Encyclopedia" />

==Behind the scenes==
Winama was first mentioned in [[Star Wars Journal: Queen Amidala|''Star Wars Episode I Journal: Queen Amidala'']], a [[1999]] young readers novel by [[Jude Watson]].<ref name="Journal" /> Her full name of Winama Naberrie was first used in the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 140|140th issue]] of [[De Agostini]]'s ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' magazine,<ref name="Fact 140" /> written by [[Iain Lowson]]<ref name="FF Author">{{LinkedIn|url=in/iainlowson|text=Iain Lowson|archivefile=File:IainLowson-LinkedIn.png}}</ref> and published around [[September 1]], [[2004]].<ref name="Date">{{FFdates|140}}</ref>
